```python
class Solution:
    def memLeak(self, memory1: int, memory2: int) -> List[int]:
        i = 1
        while max(memory1, memory2) >= i:
            if memory1 >= memory2:
                memory1 -= i
            else:
                memory2 -= i
            i += 1
        return [i, memory1, memory2]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates until the maximum available memory is less than the current time `i`, directly simulating the memory allocation process. It has a time complexity related to the number of iterations until the crash, which is proportional to the square root of the maximum initial memory due to the nature of the allocation (i bits at the i-th second).
